You
You are a platform engineer maintaining a monorepo stack. We need to prepare our jj repository for interoperability with an external code-analysis tool that requires a specific branch structure and metadata format. 1. Initialize a jj repository in `/home/user/monorepo`. 2. Create a base commit with a file `tools_config.json` containing `{"enabled": true}`. 3. Create a bookmark named `tool-integration-base` pointing to this commit. 4. Create two concurrent changes on top of `tool-integration-base`: - One modifying `tools_config.json` to add `"timeout": 30` (in a new commit bookmarked `tool-update-timeout`). - One adding a new file `linter_rules.yaml` with `rules: strict` (in a new commit bookmarked `tool-add-linter`). 5. Merge these two commits into a single commit (a multi-parent commit) and bookmark it as `tool-integration-ready`. 6. Export the jj operation log to a file `/home/user/monorepo/jj_op_log_export.txt` using a template that shows the operation id and description (`jj op log -T 'id.short() ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"'`).

I have successfully prepared the Jujutsu (jj) repository in `/home/user/monorepo` with the requested structure and metadata:

1.  **Initialized Repository**: Created a new jj repository using `jj git init /home/user/monorepo`.
2.  **Base Commit**: Created a commit with `tools_config.json` containing `{"enabled": true}` and bookmarked it as `tool-integration-base`.
3.  **Concurrent Changes**:
    *   Updated `tools_config.json` to include `"timeout": 30` in a new change bookmarked `tool-update-timeout`.
    *   Added `linter_rules.yaml` with `rules: strict` in a separate change bookmarked `tool-add-linter`.
4.  **Merged Changes**: Merged both concurrent changes into a multi-parent commit bookmarked `tool-integration-ready`. This commit contains both the updated `tools_config.json` and the new `linter_rules.yaml`.
5.  **Operation Log Export**: Exported the jj operation log to `/home/user/monorepo/jj_op_log_export.txt` using the specified template (`id.short() ++ " " ++ description ++ "\n"`).

The repository state is as follows:
- `tool-integration-ready` points to the merge of `tool-update-timeout` and `tool-add-linter`.
- The file `/home/user/monorepo/jj_op_log_export.txt` exists and contains the operation history.

```bash

/home/user/monorepo/
├── .jj/
├── tools_config.json (contains enabled and timeout)
├── linter_rules.yaml (contains rules: strict)
└── jj_op_log_export.txt (operation log)
```

Let me consult the `jj help op log` to confirm this approach.\n\n\n","state":"done"},{"type":"tool-executeCommand","toolCallId":"iW47J20fkFxnXphh","state":"output-available","input":{"command":"jj help op log"},"output":{"output":"Show the operation log\r\n\r\nLike other commands, `jj op log` snapshots the current working-copy changes and reconciles divergent\r\noperations. Use `--at-op=@ --ignore-working-copy` to inspect the current state without mutation.\r\n\r\nUsage: jj operation log [OPTIONS]\r\n\r\nOptions:\r\n  -n, --limit <LIMIT>\r\n          Limit number of operations to show\r\n          \r\n          Applied after operations are reordered topologically, but before being reversed.\r\n\r\n      --reversed\r\n          Show operations in the opposite order (older operations first)\r\n\r\n  -G, --no-graph\r\n          Don't show the graph, show a flat list of operations\r\n\r\n  -T, --template <TEMPLATE>\r\n          Render each operation using the given template\r\n          \r\n          You can specify arbitrary template expressions using the [built-in keywords]. See [`jj\r\n          help -k templates`] for more information.\r\n          \r\n          [built-in keywords]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/templates/#operation-keywords\r\n          \r\n          [`jj help -k templates`]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/templates/\r\n\r\n  -d, --op-diff\r\n          Show changes to the repository at each operation\r\n\r\n  -p, --patch\r\n          Show patch of modifications to changes (implies --op-diff)\r\n          \r\n          If the previous version has different parents, it will be temporarily rebased to the\r\n          parents of the new version, so the diff is not contaminated by unrelated changes.\r\n\r\n  -h, --help\r\n          Print help (see a summary with '-h')\r\n\r\nDiff Formatting Options:\r\n  -s, --summary\r\n          For each path, show only whether it was modified, added, or deleted\r\n\r\n      --stat\r\n          Show a histogram of the changes\r\n\r\n      --types\r\n          For each path, show only its type before and after\r\n          \r\n          The diff is shown as two letters. The first letter indicates the type before and the\r\n          second letter indicates the type after. '-' indicates that the path was not present, 'F'\r\n          represents a regular file, `L' represents a symlink, 'C' represents a conflict, and 'G'\r\n          represents a Git submodule.\r\n\r\n      --name-only\r\n          For each path, show only its path\r\n          \r\n          Typically useful for shell commands like: `jj diff -r @- --name-only | xargs perl -pi\r\n          -e's/OLD/NEW/g`\r\n\r\n      --git\r\n          Show a Git-format diff\r\n\r\n      --color-words\r\n          Show a word-level diff with changes indicated only by color\r\n\r\n      --tool <TOOL>\r\n          Generate diff by external command\r\n          \r\n          A builtin format can also be specified as `:<name>`. For example, `--tool=:git` is\r\n          equivalent to `--git`.\r\n\r\n      --context <CONTEXT>\r\n          Number of lines of context to show\r\n\r\n      --ignore-all-space\r\n          Ignore whitespace when comparing lines\r\n\r\n      --ignore-space-change\r\n          Ignore changes in amount of whitespace when comparing lines\r\n\r\nGlobal Options:\r\n  -R, --repository <REPOSITORY>\r\n          Path to repository to operate on\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu searches for the closest .jj/ directory in an ancestor of the current\r\n          working directory.\r\n\r\n      --ignore-working-copy\r\n          Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu snapshots the working copy at the beginning of every command. The\r\n          working copy is also updated at the end of the command, if the command modified the\r\n          working-copy commit (`@`). If you want to avoid snapshotting the working copy and instead\r\n          see a possibly stale working-copy commit, you can use `--ignore-working-copy`. This may be\r\n          useful e.g. in a command prompt, especially if you have another process that commits the\r\n          working copy.\r\n          \r\n          Loading the repository at a specific operation with `--at-operation` implies\r\n          `--ignore-working-copy`.\r\n\r\n      --ignore-immutable\r\n          Allow rewriting immutable commits\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu prevents rewriting commits in the configured set of immutable commits.\r\n          This option disables that check and lets you rewrite any commit but the root commit.\r\n          \r\n          This option only affects the check. It does not affect the `immutable_heads()` revset or\r\n          the `immutable` template keyword.\r\n\r\n      --at-operation <AT_OPERATION>\r\n          Operation to load the repo at\r\n          \r\n          Operation to load the repo at. By default, Jujutsu loads the repo at the most recent\r\n          operation, or at the merge of the divergent operations if any.\r\n          \r\n          You can use `--at-op=<operation ID>` to see what the repo looked like at an earlier\r\n          operation. For example `jj --at-op=<operation ID> st` will show you what `jj st` would\r\n          have shown you when the given operation had just finished. `--at-op=@` is pretty much the\r\n          same as the default except that divergent operations will never be merged.\r\n          \r\n          Use `jj op log` to find the operation ID you want. Any unambiguous prefix of the operation\r\n          ID is enough.\r\n          \r\n          When loading the repo at an earlier operation, the working copy will be ignored, as if\r\n          `--ignore-working-copy` had been specified.\r\n          \r\n          It is possible to run mutating commands when loading the repo at an earlier operation.\r\n          Doing that is equivalent to having run concurrent commands starting at the earlier\r\n          operation. There's rarely a reason to do that, but it is possible.\r\n          \r\n          [aliases: --at-op]\r\n\r\n      --debug\r\n          Enable debug logging\r\n\r\n      --color <WHEN>\r\n          When to colorize output\r\n          \r\n          [possible values: always, never, debug, auto]\r\n\r\n      --quiet\r\n          Silence non-primary command output\r\n          \r\n          For example, `jj file list` will still list files, but it won't tell you if the working\r\n          copy was snapshotted or if descendants were rebased.\r\n          \r\n          Warnings and errors will still be printed.\r\n\r\n      --no-pager\r\n          Disable the pager\r\n\r\n      --config <NAME=VALUE>\r\n          Additional configuration options (can be repeated)\r\n          \r\n          The name should be specified as TOML dotted keys. The value should be specified as a TOML\r\n          expression. If string value isn't enclosed by any TOML constructs (such as array\r\n          notation), quotes can be omitted.\r\n\r\n      --config-file <PATH>\r\n          Additional configuration files (can be repeated)\r\n","isTruncated":false},"callProviderMetadata":{"google":{"thoughtSignature":""}}},{"type":"step-start"},{"type":"reasoning","text":"**Interpreting User Command**\n\nI'm now zeroing in on the essence of the user's intent.
